title: Habakkuk's complaint and God's answer
summary_text: Habakkuk cries out over violence, injustice, and the failure of the law among his own people. The LORD answers that he is raising the Chaldeans as an instrument of judgment, a reply that shocks the prophet even more. The chapter holds together grief over sin and the unsettling truth that God can use a ruthless nation to discipline Judah.
key_points: God says the Chaldeans are coming.
The invaders are fierce and frightening.
The prophet struggles with God's answer.
evidence_phrases: I am raising up the Chaldeans
you who are of purer eyes

title: The vision, the woes, and the righteous life
summary_text: Habakkuk waits on the watchtower for God to clarify the vision, and the LORD tells him to write it plainly because it will surely come. Five woes pronounce judgment on greed, violence, exploitation, and idolatry. The chapter ends by contrasting lifeless idols with the living LORD who fills the earth with his glory.
key_points: The vision is to be written clearly.
Pride and greed bring repeated woes.
evidence_phrases: the vision is yet for the appointed time
the righteous will live by his faith
the LORD is in his holy temple

title: Habakkuk prays for mercy and rejoices in God
summary_text: Habakkuk prays that God will renew his work and then recounts the LORD's mighty march from Teman and Sinai imagery. He sees the nations trembling before God and closes by choosing joy even if crops fail and livestock vanish. The chapter turns fear into worship by resting in the God who saves.
key_points: Habakkuk asks God to revive his work.
He remembers the LORD's powerful march.
The nations shake before God's presence.
He resolves to rejoice regardless of loss.
evidence_phrases: though the fig tree doesn't blossom
I will rejoice in the LORD
